core_story.summary: Introduction Powerful new AI models[ 1, 4, 9, 12, 26, 37, 47, 48] are transforming digital image creation. Introduction Powerful new AI models[ 1, 4, 9, 12, 26, 37, 47, 48] are transforming digital image creation. Abstract As large-scale diffusion models continue to advance, they excel at producing high-quality images but often generate unwanted content, such as sexually explicit or violent im- agery. We propose a novel approach for targeted concept replacement in diffusion models, en- abling speciﬁc unwanted concepts to be removed without signiﬁcantly affecting non-target areas. Experimental results demonstrate that our method achieves superior precision in localizing target concepts and performs coherent concept replacement with minimal impact on non-target areas, outperforming existing approaches.

raw_sections.abstract:
Abstract
As large-scale diffusion models continue to advance, they
excel at producing high-quality images but often generate
unwanted content, such as sexually explicit or violent im-
agery. Existing methods for removing such content typi-
cally guide the image generation process but can inadver-
tently modify unrelated regions, leading to inconsistencies
with the original model. We propose a novel approach
for targeted concept replacement in diffusion models, en-
abling speciﬁc unwanted concepts to be removed without
signiﬁcantly affecting non-target areas. Our method intro-
duces a dedicated concept localizer for precisely identify-
ing the target concept during the denoising process, using
few-shot learning to minimize the need for labeled data.
*Corresponding authors.
Within the identiﬁed region, we further introduce a Dual
Prompts Cross-Attention module that operates without ad-
ditional training to substitute the target concept, ensuring
minimal disruption to surrounding content. We evaluate our
method in terms of concept localization precision and re-
placement efﬁciency. Experimental results demonstrate that
our method achieves superior precision in localizing target
concepts and performs coherent concept replacement with
minimal impact on non-target areas, outperforming existing
approaches. The code is available at
https://github.
com/zhang-lingyun/ConceptReplacer

1. Introduction

are transforming digital image creation. Notably, DALL-

raw_sections.method:
3. Method
Diffusion models [
19] are powerful generative models and
designed to learn data distribution p(x) by gradual denois-
ing a Gaussian distribution. Starting from sampled Gaus-
sian noise, the diffusion model gradually denoises for T
time steps to generate the ﬁnal image:
pθ (xT :0) = p(xT )
1∏
t=T
pθ (xt− 1 |xt) (1)
where p(xT ) corresponds to the initial Gaussian noise and
p(x0) corresponds to the ﬁnal generated image. Our goal is
to remove a target concept c during the denoising process of
the image p(x0) being generated.
The motivation behind our approach is to enable the
replacement of speciﬁc target concepts in diffusion mod-
els during the denoising process through precise localiza-
tion, thereby minimizing unintended effects on non-target
regions of the generated image. Existing methods for con-
cept removal often involve ﬁne-tuning the diffusion model
or introducing guidance during inference, which can inad-
vertently inﬂuence the entire output and lead to inconsis-
tencies with the original model’s behavior. To address this
issue, we propose a concept replacement method that uti-
lizes accurate localization.
As illustrated in Figure
2, our method comprises two
main components: a dedicated concept localizer and a novel
Dual Prompts Cross-Attention module. The concept local-
izer operates during the denoising process to precisely iden-
tify the target concept’s location. Subsequently, the Dual
Prompts Cross-Attention module leverages both the original
and replacing prompts to condition the image features based
on this localization information, enabling targeted concept
replacement within the speciﬁed area.
raw_sections.experiments:
4. Experiments
To validate our method, we conducted both quantitative and
qualitative analyses to evaluate the accuracy of the local-
ization of our concept localizer and the effectiveness of the
concept replacement.
raw_sections.conclusion:
5. Conclusion
In this study, we introduce Concept Replacer, a method
for replacing sensitive concepts in text-to-image diffusion
models through precise localization. Our approach employs
a few-shot trained concept localizer to accurately identify
target concepts, and a training-free Dual Prompts Cross-
Attention module uses this localization information to re-
place the target concept, ensuring that non-target regions
remain minimally affected. Our experiments demonstrate
that Concept Replacer excels in both concept localization
accuracy and replacement quality compared to existing ap-
proaches. We believe that this method can serve as a crucial
tool for generative models, enabling the effective removal of
unwanted concepts without compromising user experience.
Acknowledgment. This work was supported by the Na-
tional Key R&D Program of China(2022YFB3104300) and
the Jiangsu Provincial Natural Science Foundation of China
under Grant: BK20240291.
